methods.description: This method describes the experimental design and procedures for applying different pollination treatments to olive trees, including self-pollination, free-pollination, and cross-pollination, to study their effects on pollen viability and fertilization.

steps.description: Self-pollination (SP) was achieved by covering the fruiting branches prior to anthesis with silk paper bags. The deposition of self-pollen onto the stigmas was favored by continuously shaking the branches.
steps.searchable_summary: Cover branches with silk paper bags and shake to achieve self-pollination

steps.description: Branches subjected to free-pollination (FP) were left uncovered, receiving pollen dispersed by the wind from nearby trees.
steps.searchable_summary: Leave branches uncovered for free-pollination by wind

steps.description: For cross-pollination treatments, fresh pollen was applied manually with a fine brush to the stigmas of the open flowers.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply fresh pollen manually with a brush for cross-pollination

steps.description: Pollination was carried out at the beginning of flowering and repeated every two days on two more occasions.
steps.searchable_summary: Repeat pollination every two days during flowering

steps.description: The viability of the pollen was determined before its use by means of the fluorochromatic staining test.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine pollen viability using fluorochromatic staining test

methods.description: This method involves analyzing pollen adherence, germination, pollen tube growth, and fertilization levels in flowers subjected to different pollination treatments.

steps.description: Pollen adherence to stigma, pollen germination, pollen tube growth, and fertilization levels were quantified.
steps.searchable_summary: Quantify pollen adherence, germination, tube growth, and fertilization

steps.description: The number of pollen grains adhered to flower stigmas was estimated by counting the number of pollen grains in three small areas of the stigma.
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate adhered pollen grains by counting in stigma areas

steps.description: Pollen germination was expressed as a percentage, as the relationship between adhered and germinated pollen grains.
The growth of the pollen tube in the style of each flower was evaluated taking into account specific ranges.
steps.searchable_summary: Evaluate pollen tube growth in flower styles

steps.description: A flower was considered fertilized when there was a pollen tube in the micropyle or an ovule had become a developing seed.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine flower fertilization by pollen tube presence or seed development

methods.description: This method describes the statistical procedures used to analyze the data collected from the pollination experiments, including ANOVA and chi-square tests.

steps.description: Adhesion and germination values were analyzed using one-way analysis of variance (ANOVA) in a completely randomized design.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform ANOVA on adhesion and germination values

steps.description: Percentage data were subjected to angular transformation prior to analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply angular transformation to percentage data

steps.description: Fertilization results were analyzed using the chi-square test.
steps.searchable_summary: Conduct chi-square test on fertilization results

steps.description: The Infostat version 2017e program was used for the analyses.
